title: Guardians of the Red Earth: Bimfalb Storytellers
excerpt: Storytellers gather beneath the iroko tree where drums and oral history meet.
body:
The Bimfalb storytellers gathered beneath the iroko tree, translating winds into words.

Through drum patterns and call-and-response, history travelled across generations, reminding us that archives can also be sung.

  • Lineage chants recorded on woven leather scrolls
  • Evening fireside circles open to all ages
  • Seasonal rituals that pair story with harvest

title: Clay, Indigo, and Memory
excerpt: Artisans read the soil like a library and translate color into story.
body:
Across the Bimfalb plains, earth is pigment. Indigo dye pits and clay kilns sit side by side.

Artisans read the soil the way librarians read shelves - searching for the exact hue that tells a story.

title: When the River Carried Songs
excerpt: Boats once ferried poems; now choirs revive the route each solstice.
body:
Boats once drifted in quiet procession, ferrying poems downstream.

Today, community choirs revive that route each solstice, turning waterways into living stages.

title: Festival of Calabash Light
excerpt: Thousands of hand-carved calabashes guide communities through story and memory.
body:
Each dry season, thousands of hand-carved calabashes are lit from within and suspended above village paths.

The glow honors ancestors and guides young ones through stories of becoming.
